threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch at the sound of the sirens. Locals just carry on, as if it’s the soundtrack to daily life.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last weekend and the driver was singing along to the radio. Beats the usual awkward silence any day.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The morning I stepped outside and found two cats sunbathing on the bus stop bench, I thought, 'Yep, this is home.'

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings. You walk by one, and suddenly, it’s like stepping into another dimension.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: When someone effortlessly navigates the Muni delays while casually bemoaning the dog-on-the-tracks incident, you know they’re a local.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a musician jamming on the 38, while a guy tried to explain to his dog why it wasn't allowed to sit in the priority seat. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Parc Güell, but miss the hidden charm of Plaça de la Vila. The locals animate the square, especially during the weekly market.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their relentless use of 'por favor' and 'gràcies' when ordering coffee—locals just nod and smile.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Tube' instead of the 'Overground' when you step on at London Fields. That’ll change after a few more Sunday delays.
Last night on the 59, a guy in a chef's hat was arguing with a pigeon for the last slice of his pizza. The bird was winning.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was living in Neukölln? Walking past the corner kebab shop at 3 AM and hearing a debate about philosophy in Turkish.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you hear the clash of opera and techno at the same time during a street festival. It’s a wild cultural mashup that you just can’t predict.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You notice it when they know to step over the manholes—keeps your shoes dry during the rainy season. Only locals see that detail.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spent 20 minutes watching a Muni driver coax a therapy pig off the bus. Only in the TL, right? That's our city in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s plazas are for sipping coffee, but locals know they’re just a meeting point before the real fun starts around the corner.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their wide-eyed wonder at the Fira de Gràcia. Most locals just roll their eyes at the crowds and overpriced churros.
